svg.text: Option C — exterior stair · maximum apartment
Covered stair on the east face · all 437 sf net stays inside · kitchen earns its keep on the blank north wall
north ↑ · property line 3' — keep this wall (nearly) window-free
south · shed 4' away · best all-day sun
east · yard & main house · morning sun
kitchen / dining · 8'-6" run on the blank wall
LEVEL 2 — apartment · 480 sf gross / 437 sf net
north ↑ · property line 3' — keep this wall (nearly) window-free
south · shed 4' away · best all-day sun
east · yard & main house · morning sun
• PRO: ≈46 sf more apartment AND ≈46 sf more garage than A/B; kitchen sits on the windowless north wall where nothing else wants to be.
• PRO: garage stays one clean 23' x 19' volume (shop + car + bigger office corner); apartment and garage are fully separate (good if you ever rent one).
• CON: you arrive outside — groceries, rain, ice; the stair + landing add bulk to the yard-facing facade and shade the east windows below.
• CON: harder aging-in-place story; exterior stairs need real detailing (covered, lit, guard) to not look tacked-on.
